zh.East Peckham, Kent, England: 英國肯特易亞斯特 比可哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Ida de Hastings於1212年出生在英國諾福爾可阿希爾時，她的父親Henry de Hastings16歲，母親Ada Huntingdon17歲。她和peckham至少有1個兒子。她去世於1288年3月2日之前，埋葬在英國米德萊可倫敦。

zh-hans.East Peckham, Kent, England: 英国肯特易亞斯特 比可哈姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ida de Hastings于1212年出生在英国諾福爾可阿希爾时，她的父亲Henry de Hastings16岁，母亲Ada Huntingdon17岁。她和peckham至少有1个儿子。她去世于1288年3月2日之前，埋葬在英国米德萊可倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hastings
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from Hastings (Sussex), near which the English army was defeated by the Normans in 1066. The placename derives from an Old English personal name Hǣsta + the Old English groupname suffix -ingas. In the latter part of the 12th century, at the time of William the Lion, the surname was taken to Scotland, where it assimilated instances of the native Scottish surname Harestane.
English: variant of Hasting with Middle English genitival -s.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hOistín ‘descendant of Oistín’, the Gaelic form of the personal name Augustine (see Austin ).

variants.name: Hasting
meanings: English: from the Norman personal name Hastang, Hastenc, Hasten (Old Norse Hásteinn ‘high’ + ‘stone’). See also Hastings .

variants.name: Heston
meanings: English: habitational name from Heston (Middlesex) or Histon (Cambridgeshire). The Middlesex placename derives from Old English hǣs ‘brushwood’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, while the Cambridgeshire placename may derive from Old English hyse ‘shoot, tendril’, also ‘son, youth’, + tūn.

nameMeanings.name: Ida
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Norman name, of Germanic origin, derived from īd ‘work’. This died out during the later Middle Ages. It was revived in the 19th century, influenced by its use in Tennyson's The Princess ( 1847 ) for the central character, who devotes herself to the cause of women's rights and women's education in a thoroughly Victorian way. The name is also associated with Mount Ida in Crete, which was connected in classical times with the worship of Zeus, king of the gods, who was supposed to have been brought up in a cave on the mountainside. In the 1930s it became famous as the name of the film star Ida Lupino ( 1914–1995 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Ida de Hastings was born in 1212, in Ashill, Norfolk, England, her father, Henry de Hastings, was 16 and her mother, Ada Huntingdon, was 17. She had at least 1 son with peckham. She died before 2 March 1288, and was buried in London, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ida de Hastings est née en 1212, à Ashill, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Henry de Hastings, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Ada Huntingdon, avait 17 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec peckham. Elle est décédée avant 2 mars 1288, et a été enterrée à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ida de Hastings 1212 in Ashill, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry de Hastings 16 und ihre Mutter Ada Huntingdon 17. Sie hatte mit peckham mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb vor 2. März 1288 und wurde in London, Middlesex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ida de Hastings è nata il nel 1212 nel Ashill, Norfolk, England, sua padre, Henry de Hastings, aveva 16 anni e sua madre, Ada Huntingdon, aveva 17 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con peckham. Morì prima del 2 marzo 1288, e fu sepolta a London, Middlesex, England.

ja.Ashill, Norfolk, England: イギリスノーフォルクアシュイル
ja.lifeSummary: Ida de Hastingsが1212年にイギリスノーフォルクアシュイルで生まれた時，父Henry de Hastingsは16歳，母Ada Huntingdonは17歳であった。彼女はpeckhamとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1288年3月2日以前に死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ida de Hastings(이)가 1212년 영국 노르폴크 애시에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry de Hastings(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ada Huntingdon(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 peckham(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1288년 3월 2일 이전 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ida de Hastings nasceu em 1212, em Ashill, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry de Hastings, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Ada Huntingdon, tinha 17 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com peckham. Ela faleceu antes 2 de março de 1288, e foi sepultada em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ida de Hastings родилась в 1212 г. в местности ашилл, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Henry de Hastings было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Ada Huntingdon, 17. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от peckham. Она умерла перед 2 марта 1288 г. и была похоронена в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ida de Hastings nació en 1212, en Ashill, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry de Hastings, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Ada Huntingdon, tenía 17 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con peckham. Murió antes de 2 de marzo de 1288, y fue sepultada en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
